_provenance.source: Hand-authored. Bounded vocabulary for JclValidationResult. Mirrors ErrorCategory + Severity enums in flow_ml/data/schemas.py.
_provenance.v2_change: Added `severities: [..., none]` entry so the classifier can emit 'none' alongside the 'none' error code on clean JCL. Added `error_message_templates` so the v2 classifier-only path can fill `message` + `suggestion` from the predicted code without generating text (zero-hallucination guarantee).

valid_when.rationale: If `valid: true`, the `errors` array MUST be empty. If `valid: false`, the array MUST contain at least one entry.

confidence.calibration_note: 0.9+ on clean valid samples; 0.7-0.9 on confident error detections; <0.7 when ambiguous.

error_message_templates_purpose: The v2 classifier predicts a code from the bounded set but does not generate text. The runtime fills `message` + `suggestion` from these templates keyed by the predicted code. Zero hallucination on user-facing copy.

missing_dd.message: Required DD statement is missing for this step.
missing_dd.suggestion: Add the DD statement (e.g. `//SYSPRINT DD SYSOUT=*` or the dataset-specific DD) before the next EXEC.

invalid_job_card.message: JOB card is malformed (account, class, msgclass, or priority fields are missing or invalid).
invalid_job_card.suggestion: Re-check the JOB statement: accounting field in parentheses, then optional CLASS / MSGCLASS / PRIORITY operands.

unresolved_symbolic_parameter.message: A symbolic parameter (e.g. &NAME) is referenced but never set.
unresolved_symbolic_parameter.suggestion: Define the symbolic via SET before its first use, or replace the reference with a literal value.

continuation_error.message: JCL continuation line does not follow the column-72 / column-16 continuation rules.
continuation_error.suggestion: Ensure the continued line starts at column 16, and the previous line's column-72 holds a non-blank continuation marker.

invalid_exec_statement.message: EXEC statement is malformed (missing PGM= or PROC=, or operands in wrong order).
invalid_exec_statement.suggestion: Use `//STEPNAME EXEC PGM=program-name` or `//STEPNAME EXEC PROC=proc-name`.

invalid_dataset_reference_structure.message: Dataset reference (DSN=) does not parse as a valid z/OS dataset name.
invalid_dataset_reference_structure.suggestion: Use qualified names with dots (e.g. `DSN=PROD.DAILY.LOAD`); each qualifier is 1-8 alphanumeric chars starting with A-Z or @#$.

other.message: JCL statement is invalid for a reason outside the standard error categories.
other.suggestion: Open the spool / SDSF view for the full message and review the JCL against the IBM z/OS JCL Reference.
